text: Build a Hunter x Hunter character by choosing your childhood, lifepath, motivation, aura, Nen affinity, core powers, limitations, and long-term skill development. This adaptation is based on the three source pages you provided and keeps their overall structure while fitting the JSON format used by this project.

subcategories.name: Childhood
subcategories.text: Pick one childhood. These are broad origin tones drawn from the first page.

options.label: Comfy
options.description: A stable, ordinary upbringing. You were not especially privileged or hardened, but you had room to grow normally.

options.label: Miserable
options.description: Hardship shaped you early. Poverty, danger, neglect, or violence left a mark and pushed you to survive.

options.label: Secluded
options.description: You grew up isolated from the wider world, perhaps in a remote settlement, temple, forest, or mountain community.

options.label: Socialite
options.description: You were raised around wealth, status, and connections. You learned how class, reputation, and influence shape people's lives.

subcategories.name: Lifepath
subcategories.text: Pick one primary lifepath and optionally a second one to reflect side work or prior training.

options.label: Ruins Hunter
options.description: You explore archaeological sites, recover relics, and study vanished civilizations.

options.label: Beast Hunter
options.description: You track rare and dangerous creatures, documenting or hunting them in the wild.

options.label: Blacklist Hunter
options.description: You hunt wanted criminals and dangerous targets, often taking legally gray contracts.

options.label: Botanical Hunter
options.description: You travel to gather rare plants, medicines, and natural specimens from distant regions.

options.label: Crime Hunter
options.description: You investigate mysterious cases and pursue criminals through deduction and fieldwork.

options.label: Sea Hunter
options.description: You work on or beneath the water, chasing aquatic treasures, creatures, and maritime secrets.

options.label: Gourmet Hunter
options.description: You seek rare foods and ingredients, turning cuisine into a full profession and adventure.

options.label: Sex Hunter
options.description: An eccentric and provocative profession centered on pursuing unusual beauty and rare partners.

options.label: Hacker Hunter
options.description: You specialize in the digital world, handling cybercrime, security, and hidden information.

options.label: Head Hunter
options.description: You search for exceptional people and talent, often scouting or organizing high-level applicants.

options.label: Music Hunter
options.description: You devote yourself to rare music, musicians, and dangerous sound-related artifacts or phenomena.

options.label: Poacher Hunter
options.description: You protect wildlife and natural territories from poachers, often in harsh environments.

options.label: Art Hunter
options.description: You recover lost masterpieces and protect or broker priceless works with dangerous histories.

options.label: Virus Hunter
options.description: You study outbreaks, diseases, and biological threats directly in the field.

options.label: Lost Hunter
options.description: You specialize in finding people, places, and things that have disappeared.

options.label: Poison Hunter
options.description: You research toxins, venoms, antidotes, and the creatures or plants that produce them.

options.label: Terrorist Hunter
options.description: You are trained to track and neutralize extremist threats through direct action.

options.label: Treasure Hunter
options.description: You scour the world for buried wealth, forgotten relics, and lost vaults.

options.label: Temp Hunter
options.description: You work flexible contracts for the Hunter Association, taking whatever jobs need doing.

options.label: Rookie
options.description: You lack a clear specialization and are still trying to discover what kind of hunter you will become.

options.label: Soldier
options.description: You served in a national military force and are used to dangerous operations.

options.label: Martial Artist
options.description: You travel to test and refine your craft against other fighters.

options.label: Heaven Arena
options.description: You built your career in Heaven's Arena, fighting opponents floor by floor.

options.label: Mercenary
options.description: You worked for hire in conflicts and unstable regions, selling skill rather than loyalty.

options.label: Monk
options.description: You were raised in a disciplined spiritual life of prayer, meditation, and self-cultivation.

options.label: Criminal
options.description: You moved through the underworld, either alone or as part of a larger organization.

options.label: Assassin
options.description: You are a professional killer or were trained as one from an early age.

options.label: Civilian
options.description: On paper you look ordinary, but Nen gives your otherwise normal life unusual depth.

subcategories.name: Motivation
subcategories.text: Why are you on this path?

options.label: Adventure
options.description: You want freedom, travel, new experiences, and the thrill of a larger world.

options.label: Tragedy
options.description: Loss or suffering forced you forward and continues to define your goals.

options.label: Obsession
options.description: There is something specific you must understand, obtain, defeat, or reach.

options.label: Pragmatism
options.description: This path solves practical problems: money, safety, influence, or leverage.

options.label: Boredom
options.description: You wanted intensity, novelty, and something difficult enough to feel alive.

subcategories.name: Vibe
subcategories.text: Pick the aura your style gives off.

options.label: Cute
options.description: Light, friendly, and approachable on the surface.

options.label: Classy
options.description: Refined, polished, and sharply presented.

options.label: Badass
options.description: Confident, dangerous, and impossible to ignore.

options.label: Sinister
options.description: A threatening or uncanny air hangs around you.

options.label: Serene
options.description: Calm, composed, and difficult to disturb.

options.label: Goofy
options.description: Chaotic, expressive, and hard to predict.

subcategories.name: Nen Category
subcategories.text: Pick your natural Nen affinity.

options.label: Enhancer
options.description: Simple and direct. Enhancers reinforce the body, objects, or a specific trait with straightforward force.

options.label: Emitter
options.description: Fast and explosive. Emitters excel at projecting aura away from the body.

options.label: Transmuter
options.description: Tricksy and expressive. Transmuters alter aura so it imitates qualities or becomes a usable substance.

options.label: Conjurer
options.description: Careful and detail-oriented. Conjurers create stable objects, spaces, or systems out of aura.

options.label: Manipulator
options.description: Methodical and controlling. Manipulators impose their will through a medium or target.

options.label: Specialist
options.description: Rare and abnormal. Specialists break the standard rules and often revolve around singular talents.

subcategories.name: Core Powers
subcategories.text: Pick one or two core powers. They are nested by Nen category to match the source image. If you take two, imagine them mixing into a single Hatsu concept.

subcategories.name: Enhancement
subcategories.text: Enhancement applications focus on directly reinforcing the body, tools, or a specific trait.

options.label: Body Enhancement
options.description: Directly reinforce your physical power, speed, and toughness.

options.label: Object Enhancement
options.description: Strengthen a tool, weapon, or item so it performs far beyond normal limits.

options.label: Self Enhancement
options.description: Enhance a specific personal trait or function such as healing, senses, or resilience.

subcategories.name: Emission
subcategories.text: Emission applications project aura outward or displace things through aura over distance.

options.label: Aura Projection
options.description: Launch aura as blasts, shots, beams, or other ranged techniques.

options.label: Nen-made Creatures
options.description: Create aura beasts, constructs, or familiars that can act on your behalf.

options.label: Teleportation
options.description: Shift yourself, objects, or others through space by aura-based displacement.

subcategories.name: Transmutation
subcategories.text: Transmutation applications alter aura so it mimics qualities or becomes a shaped substance.

options.label: Quality Imitation
options.description: Give your aura the physical or chemical properties of something else.

options.label: Aura Sculpture
options.description: Solidify and shape aura into practical forms such as blades, limbs, or constructs.

subcategories.name: Conjuration
subcategories.text: Conjuration applications create stable objects, spaces, or transformations through aura.

options.label: Object Summoning
options.description: Conjure useful items, tools, weapons, or special devices into existence.

options.label: Pocket Dimension
options.description: Create a separate space with unique rules, storage, or entry conditions.

options.label: Polymorphism
options.description: Use conjuration or conjured tools to alter shape, form, or function.

subcategories.name: Manipulation
subcategories.text: Manipulation applications control bodies, objects, or actions through your aura.

options.label: Forced Control
options.description: Take direct control of bodies, objects, or actions through your aura.

options.label: Puppeteering
options.description: Issue commands through prepared mediums, dolls, objects, or marked targets.

subcategories.name: Specialization
subcategories.text: Specialization applications cover unusual or singular effects that do not fit cleanly elsewhere.

options.label: Ability Theft
options.description: Steal, borrow, store, or temporarily use powers that normally belong to others.

options.label: Unclassifiable Effect
options.description: A specialist effect that does something the standard categories do not cleanly explain.

subcategories.name: Limitations
subcategories.text: Pick the intensity of your restrictions. Stronger vows can produce stronger effects, but most hunters keep them modest.

options.label: None
options.description: Your ability works with little or no special cost, ritual, or restriction.

options.label: Minor
options.description: Your Hatsu needs a manageable condition, gesture, item, or setup step.

options.label: Heavy
options.description: Your Hatsu relies on serious vows or strict activation rules with real tactical impact.

options.label: Extreme
options.description: Your Hatsu is tied to dangerous all-or-nothing conditions with brutal consequences for failure.

subcategories.name: Strength
subcategories.text: Strength encompasses your physical power, your musculature, your stamina, the robustness of your body and its ability to withstand impacts, and in general, your ability to use your physical strength to get your out of any kind of situation.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: The standard physical strength of an average nen user. Well above the average human average, correct for a manipulator or conjurer, but far below the strength of your typical enhancer.

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: The strength of a manipulator with a particular developed physics or a mid-level transmuter. At this point, you are capable of lifting several tons, killing an ordinary human being with a single punch of surviving gun shots (even if it can hurt you)

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: The kind of strength that very few manipulators and conjurers possess, and which makes you very dangerous in hand to hand combat for the majority of nen users. If you particupated in the Phantom Troupe arm wrestling, you would probably end up in 5th or 6th place like Feitan or Machi. Also, you are now able to tank bullets with relatively low damage, or none at all.

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: Now you are in the territory of the super strong, super capable of astonishing physical feats even for nen users. Think of Razor's ball throwing or the physical strength of Hisoka and Phinks who can decapitate a man barehanded.

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: You are borderline inhuman, probably one of the most powerful enhancers in the world, able, like Uvogin, to send nen users flying with your punches or tank rockets without problem.

subcategories.name: Vigilance
subcategories.text: Sensitivity to danger, awareness, and the acuity of your senses.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: You are not particularly perceptive, maybe you even lack a bit of it compared to other nen users (think of Leorio who is the only one who does not feel Alluka's aura during the elections.)

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: You have a slightly sharper sensitivity than the average nen user. You keep a constant attention on your environment and are able to spot minute details around you.

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: Your perception is particularly developed. You are perfectly capable of knowing when someone is watching you from afar, or even guessing where the faze is coming from, even in the middle of a crowd, and only a very good zetsu can deceive your senses.

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: You have the sensitivity of a nen master. Not only is a perfect zetsu capable of deceiving you completely (like Hisoka who feels Kalluto's presence despite his flawless zetsu) but you are also able to deduce with approximate precision the strength of a nen user just by observing his aura.

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: Your sensitivity is verging on the inhuman. You would spot a fly flying a hundred yards away and feel the slightest nen disturbance kilometers away. A single look at a nen user gives you a very precise idea of their abilities, even their emotions.

subcategories.name: Reaction
subcategories.text: Reaction covers your reactivity, your reflexes, your reaction time when faced with a situation that requires you to respond quickly like dodging or parrying an attack or catching something that is flying at you. This is an absolutely essential characteristic for a nen user, especially for a fighter.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: Your responsiveness is a little behind the average nen user. You are one of those somewhat slow nen users who can be outspeeded fairly easily. Although, it is not that disadvantaging if you are a non-combatant.

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: This is the speed needed to quality as a fighter and exceed the reflexes of non-combatatnt nen users. You are able to see most blows coming and react at least 5 times faster than a professional boxer.

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: Most nen users with this kind of reflexes are combat veterans, able to react even to surprise attacks or gunfire (like Furykov who manages to protect his head when Camilia shoots him unexpectedly)

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: At this point, you are capable of insane feats of agility and reactivity, such as following the trajectory of a blow faster than asound with your eyes (Morel and Knuckle against Cheetah) or dodging incredibly fast projectiles even at close range. (Biscuit and Hisoka dodging Razor's ball)

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: Nen users with this kind of reactivity have an almost instantaneous reaction time and are able to compress their perception of time where everything seems to be frozen around them. Think Pitou against Netero.

subcategories.name: Finesse
subcategories.text: Basically your motor skills and technique, the dexterity with which you use your body and hands, the precision of your moves, your coordination. A characteristic particularly developed in fighters and even more so in assassins and martial artists.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: You have almost surgical control over your movements. But that is the norm for any mid-level nen user and it is not something exceptional for people like yourself.

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: You have a particularly sharp manual dexterity and quasi-mechanical movements when it comes to performing certain tasks. You are able to throw a dart into the centre of a target at least 9 out of 10 times at a distance of 20 meters.

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: Your dexterity is that of a professional assassins or a kung fu master who has trained for years. Ripping someone's heart out without making their wound bleed like Killua, or catching a fly in mid-air with chopsticks is not even difficult.

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: Now, your finesse is that of master assassins and elite fighters, not only in terms of dexterity and precision, but also in stealth. Shooting a projectile into a tiny target hundreds of meters away (Uvogin who kills a sniper with a stone throw), completely erasing your presence to kill someone from behind with a single touch in a vial spot (Chrollo against mafia hitmen). All this is possible for you.

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: Your dexterity is legendary. You could pickpocket from a nen user on his guard, even when being watched by his comrades without anyone noticing you. You have probably mastered the most advanced and lethal martial arts techniques such as Biscuit or Netero.

subcategories.name: Willpower
subcategories.text: Willpower has an influence on many more aspects than mere determination. It is also the aptitude to remain focused and lucid in the face of danger and in very tense situations. It is also the pain and fatigue tolerance, the guts to not run away from a much strong opponent, etc.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: It is pretty obvious you are not really used to violence. You are braver than an ordinary human, but a powerful or creepy aura will paralyze you with terror.

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: The kind of willpower needed to undertake a dangerous job in which you risk your life. In spite of the panic, you are able to keep thinking rationally and you can stand the sensation of a terrifying aura without losing control of yourself.

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: You have nerves of steel. You are capable of resisting torture or keeping calm in the face of death, like a veteran such as Morel. It will take a lot to intimidate you.

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: It is not really only that you can remain calm in the face of life-threatening danger, you are perfectly indifferent to the the idea of dying like Chrollo. Moreover, your resistance to pain is borderline inhuman and you can brush off the pain of a severed arm with a smile like Hisoka.

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: Your mind is like a gigantic and unmovable mountain. Your willpower is identical to that of Gon during the assault on the palace. Your gaze is enough to paralyze your enemies with feat at the thought of your absurd determination.

subcategories.name: Intelligence
subcategories.text: The type of intelligence that allows for long-term planning or the elaboration of complex strategies before a fight, the deduction of sensitive information such as your opponent's ability form very few elements, guessing someone's next moves in advance, etc.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: It's not that you are dumb, it is just that thinking is not your forte. Thinking about complex things quickly overheats your brain and you prefer simple and clear situations.

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: You are not a genius but you are able to set up reasonably complex strategies when you have enough time and resources. That is somewhat above average intelligence for a human being.

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: You can be described as particularly smart and resourceful. You are  a Killua-level strategist, much cleverer and way more intuitive than most nen users. You can follow the conversation of brilliant military tacticians or cunning politicians.

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: You are a genius and a planner with an extremely sharp mind like Chrollo or Kurapika. You are able to handle various extremely complex and convoluted schemes simultaneously or see through the most twisted political plots.

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: It is the intelligence of the most powerful brains in the world of HunterxHunter like Ging and Pariston. Predicting someone's actions years in advance or manipulating entire factions for fun are feats you perform regularly. Your mind has the computing power of a computer.

subcategories.name: Quick-Thinking
subcategories.text: It is not only the ability to think quickly, to instantly improvise a tactic in the middle of a fight, to exploit your immediate environment to trick your opponent or develop a strategy, but it is also your creativity, the agility of your mind and your ability to think outside the box.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: Average intelligence in humans. Like most people, you are completely lost in a nen fight. It is likely you are a non-combatant because most fighters can outsmart you easily.

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: Your mind works fast, more than average anyways. If there are particularly exploitable features in your environment, you know how to use them to improvise on the spot rather than ingenious and efficient tactics.

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: Not only does you mind work quickly and efficiently, but you also think in a particularly unconventional way. You strategies are surprising and this is a very important quality in a nen battle to prevent your opponent from predicting your actions.

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: You are on the same level as a particularly smart combat veteran like Morel. You can think of dozens of different ways to use your ability and exploit tiny weaknesses in your opponent. It is as if you always have multiple tactics in mind, allowing you to adapt to any type of situation or environment.

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: You are a combat genius like Hisoka. It is impossible to follow your thoughts. A tiny detail around you is enough to come up with a totally new strategy, almost instantly, so complex and unconventional that an ordinary human being would find it difficult to figure it out with time and resources.

subcategories.name: Nen Mastery
subcategories.text: This characteristic reflects the depth of your knowledge of nen and its most advanced aspects, but also the dexterity and precision with which you control your aura. This is ultimately what differentiates a master with real understanding of nen from someone who simply makes practical usage of it.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: You master bery well the basic principles of nen (Ten, Ren, Zetsu, Hatsu) but have a superficial knowledge of nen but this does not really go beyond what a rather talented novice knows.

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: You mater most advanced aspects of nen such as In, Ryu, Ken, or Ko. Moreover, your zetsu is really high level and you demonstrate essential reflexes in terms of nen mastery (such as instantly using Gyo as easily as you breathe when you fight or maintaining Ren for 30 minutes without being exhausted). That is the same level of mastery as Gon and Killua facing Knuckle.

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: Now you are starting to master your aura in really subtle way like a combat veteran of the caliber of Knuckle or Kite. You can deceive your opponent by making you aura follow complex and deceptive movements or close and open your shokos [sic] at lightning speed as Tsezugarra is able to do so.

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: At this point, you are what most would call a grand master of nen like Biscuit. You have a perfectly balanced mastery of all categories of nen, even those that are far from your natural category, and you control your aura with surgical precision. You are probably familiar with extremely advanced notions of nen that have not even been introduced to the manga yet.

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: This is the level of nen comprehension that only a few rare absolute geniuses like Ging can reach, capable of creating new abilities on the fly, almost naturally. You aura is completely quiet. Your mastery of Ten even allows you to slow down your aging process like Netero.

subcategories.name: Aura Pool
subcategories.text: Aura is the fuel for any nen attack and this charactistic reflects the amount of aura you possess and are able to deploy in combat. A large pool allows you to fight for a long time without getting exhausted, but also to intimidate your opponent by the simple size of your Ren.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: Your aura pool is slightly below average and you burn out quickly when you use your ability. You probably lack training or regularity in basic exercises.

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: You have the same amount of aura as amid level nen user which is about the same as Gon on Greed Island. That is the minimum needed to call yourself a fighter.

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: You have the same amount of aura as a top fighter like Knuckle. This allows you to use your ability in many different ways and to focus much more aura in your fists and feet when striking your opponent or protecting yourself.

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: Not only is your aura pool the same as that of an accomplished nen master like Morel, who can keep his ability activated for days, but the size and intensity of your ren even allows you to intimidate and terrify low-level opponents without having to fight like Razor does.

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: Now the amount of aura you possess makes your opponent doubt you are human. Your energy reserves seems impossible to exhaust. Facing your Ren is like facing a huge mountain of aura.

subcategories.name: Quirk
subcategories.text: This characteristic is something that few people possess. A quirk is a physical feature or special talent that is unique to you or almost unique to you and that is difficult or even impossible for someone else to acquire through training. Most of the time, it is something you are born with.

options.label: Tier 0
options.description: Like almost everyone else, you do not have a particular quirk. There's nothing disadvantageous about it, and that is the case for the vast majority of characters in HunterxHunter.

options.label: Tier 1
options.description: You have a minor quirk. It can be a special skill or a slight physiological abnormality that sets you apart from the rest of the population and is rather useful on a daily basis, such as Gon's highly developed sense of smell.

options.label: Tier 2
options.description: Your quirk is a little more important and can have extremely useful applications in certain situations. It can even have great synergy with your nen ability. In this category, we cna amention Killua's retractable claws or Morel's superhuman lungs.

options.label: Tier 3
options.description: You have a major quirk, probably unique in the world, which you use all the time and which has already saved your life on many occasions. This is the case, for example, with Melody's overdeveloped hearing or Zoldyck's immunity to poisons.

options.label: Tier 4
options.description: This kind of quirk is on the same level as Ging's natural genius for manipulating nen and his natural birth skill to be able to reproduce any simple ability that touched him once. But HOLD ON: This quirk is just too broken for everyone to have it, even at the cost of 4 points. If you want to buy this tier, roll 1d6. On a 6, you CAN buy it for 4 points. On any other result, you cannot buy it. Too bad, life is unfair.
